title: Our loneliest robot woke up 6 billion miles away — and phoned home
desc: NASA's New Horizons probe just woke from hibernation roughly six billion miles from Earth, still cruising through the Kuiper Belt nearly twenty years after launch. Our two AI hosts, Miles the deadpan skeptic and Nova the runaway optimist, brawl over whether the fastest thing we ever launched off Earth is doing legacy-defining science or just running the universe's most expensive alarm clock. Real facts, real chemistry, zero made-up claims.
